background.purpose: Page background. Near-white with a faint warm cast. Reads as premium paper, not OS desktop white.

canvas-top.purpose: Pure white. Used for cards floating on the canvas and as the top stop of the foot-glow gradient.

canvas-foot-tint.purpose: Warm-tinted base of the foot-glow gradient. Never a solid fill — only the bottom stop.

surface.purpose: Card and modal background. Pure white earns its place because elevation says this content is the focus.

surface-muted.purpose: Subtle inset fills, hover affordances, table header rows.

surface-sunken.purpose: Nested panels and demo-data flag chips. The lowest neutral allowed on a surface.

text-primary.purpose: Body and headings. Warm graphite, never pure black.

accent.purpose: Brand accent. Used at full saturation only on small surfaces — dots, gradient top-stops, soft-fill pills. NOT on CTA buttons (those use ink-pill).

accent-active.purpose: The only place clay shows up on a button — as the hover state of the ink-pill primary CTA.

accent-soft.purpose: Tinted background for selected pills and chart soft fills.

ink-pill.purpose: Primary CTA background. Semantically distinct from text-primary (it's a surface, not text). Hover deepens to accent-active (clay).

secondary.purpose: Second-string neutral. Pipeline-stage badges for human-touch states (sold, installing).

status-on-track.purpose: Health: on-track. Lean semantic; never used for non-status purposes.

status-at-risk.purpose: Health: at-risk. Warm amber, not safety-yellow.

status-blocked.purpose: Health: blocked. Dusty red, not alarm-red.

status-complete.purpose: Done and stowed, not a victory.

status-andon.purpose: The ONLY loud color in the system. Reserved for active andon alerts and critical safety. Pulses at 1200ms.

display.purpose: Cormorant Garamond 500. The morning Good morning, Andrew line and any single-statement hero copy. Italic available for in-line emphasis on 1-2 words.

headline.purpose: Cormorant Garamond 500 at 24-28px. Used by PageHeader on every route. Replaces the previous Inter 24px heading.

title.purpose: Cormorant Garamond 500 at 18px. Section heads inside a page, card heads. Examples: This week's hitlist, Shop capacity this week.

body.purpose: Inter 400 at 14px. Default body and table cells. Slightly tighter letter-spacing than web norm.

body-emphasis.purpose: Inter 500 at 14px. Hitlist row next-step sentence; primary text in interactive list rows.

label.purpose: Inter 500 at 11px uppercase +0.06em. Section dividers, eyebrow text, demo tags.

mono.purpose: JetBrains Mono at 13px for prices, percentages, job codes. Tabular-nums applied via utility class.

shadows.name: resting
shadows.purpose: Default soft lift on cards at rest. Subtle but present. Cards never use borders to separate from canvas; this shadow does the work.

shadows.name: hover
shadows.purpose: Draggable cards, focused cards, Hitlist hero. Deepens by ~80% from resting. Don't introduce color on hover — introduce lift.

shadows.name: floating
shadows.purpose: Segmented pill, command palette base. Multi-layered soft shadow that signals 'persistently afloat'.

shadows.name: modal
shadows.purpose: Modals, dropdowns, andon banners. Reserved for true elevation; cards don't get to use this.

motion.name: ease-standard
motion.purpose: Default easing for state transitions. Short, eased, physical — Apple/Claude-feel.

motion.name: ease-emphasized
motion.purpose: Larger entrances (modal, drawer, page transitions). Slightly more pronounced curve.

motion.name: duration-base
motion.purpose: Modal enter, drawer slide, page fade.

motion.name: duration-slow
motion.purpose: Reserved for transitions that need to land deliberately (e.g., view-switch flourish if introduced).

motion.name: andon-pulse
motion.purpose: The ONE sustained animation in the entire product. Used only on active andon alerts. Never on decorative elements.

breakpoints.name: lg
breakpoints.purpose: Small laptop. Sidebar expands from icon rail to full.

breakpoints.name: tv
breakpoints.purpose: Shop-floor wall display. Full-bleed mode, +1 type size, no sidebar.

components.name: Primary CTA — Ink Pill
components.description: The dark ink-pill primary affordance. Hover deepens to clay-active. The brand's most-used button.
components.css: .ds-btn-primary { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; gap: 6px; background: var(--ink-pill, #1A1916); color: #FFFFFF; border: 0; padding: 8px 16px; border-radius: 999px; font: 500 14px/1.4 var(--font-inter, 'Inter', system-ui, sans-serif); letter-spacing: -0.005em; cursor: pointer; transition: background 120ms cubic-bezier(0.2, 0, 0, 1); } .ds-btn-primary:hover { background: var(--accent-active, #8F4F36); } .ds-btn-primary:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--accent, #B86F52); outline-offset: 2px; }

components.name: Secondary Button
components.description: White, no border, soft floating shadow. Used for Cancel / Back / secondary actions next to a primary CTA.
components.css: .ds-btn-secondary { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; gap: 6px; background: #FFFFFF; color: var(--text-primary, #1A1916); border: 0; padding: 8px 16px; border-radius: 999px; font: 500 14px/1.4 var(--font-inter, 'Inter', system-ui, sans-serif); letter-spacing: -0.005em; cursor: pointer; box-shadow: 0 1px 2px rgba(26,25,22,0.04), 0 6px 18px -10px rgba(26,25,22,0.10); transition: box-shadow 120ms cubic-bezier(0.2, 0, 0, 1); } .ds-btn-secondary:hover { box-shadow: 0 1px 2px rgba(26,25,22,0.04), 0 8px 22px -14px rgba(26,25,22,0.18); }

components.name: Segmented Pill (ViewToggle)
components.description: Translucent white container with backdrop-blur, holding 2-4 mutually-exclusive options. Active option becomes a small dark ink-pill.
components.css: .ds-segmented { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; gap: 2px; background: rgba(255,255,255,0.7); backdrop-filter: blur(8px); -webkit-backdrop-filter: blur(8px); border-radius: 999px; padding: 4px; box-shadow: 0 1px 2px rgba(26,25,22,0.04), 0 6px 18px -10px rgba(26,25,22,0.10); } .ds-seg-btn { background: transparent; border: 0; padding: 6px 12px; border-radius: 999px; font: 500 12px/1.4 var(--font-inter, 'Inter', system-ui, sans-serif); color: var(--text-secondary, #4F4D49); cursor: pointer; transition: color 120ms cubic-bezier(0.2, 0, 0, 1); } .ds-seg-btn:hover { color: var(--text-primary, #1A1916); } .ds-seg-active, .ds-seg-active:hover { background: var(--ink-pill, #1A1916); color: #FFFFFF; }

components.name: Card
components.description: White surface, no border, soft resting shadow. Cards earn separation through tonal step against the canvas plus the shadow.
components.html:
This week's hitlist

8 jobs need you. Two are blocked.
components.css: .ds-card { background: #FFFFFF; border-radius: 14px; padding: 20px; box-shadow: 0 8px 22px -14px rgba(26,25,22,0.10); border: 0; } .ds-card-title { margin: 0 0 6px; font: 500 18px/1.3 var(--font-cormorant, 'Cormorant Garamond', Georgia, serif); letter-spacing: -0.01em; color: var(--text-primary, #1A1916); } .ds-card-body { margin: 0; font: 400 14px/1.55 var(--font-inter, 'Inter', system-ui, sans-serif); color: var(--text-secondary, #4F4D49); letter-spacing: -0.01em; }

components.name: Input
components.description: Solid background, 1px border, soft focus ring. The one place borders survive the no-line doctrine.
components.css: .ds-input-row { display: block; } .ds-input-label { display: block; margin-bottom: 4px; font: 500 12px/1.4 var(--font-inter, 'Inter', system-ui, sans-serif); color: var(--text-secondary, #4F4D49); } .ds-input { display: block; width: 100%; background: #FFFFFF; color: var(--text-primary, #1A1916); border: 1px solid var(--border, #E2DFD9); border-radius: 6px; padding: 8px 12px; font: 400 14px/1.55 var(--font-inter, 'Inter', system-ui, sans-serif); letter-spacing: -0.01em; transition: border-color 120ms, box-shadow 120ms; } .ds-input:focus { outline: 0; border-color: var(--border-strong, #CDC9C1); box-shadow: 0 0 0 2px var(--accent-soft, #F2E4DC); } .ds-input::placeholder { color: var(--text-tertiary, #8B8782); }

components.name: Health Pill — At Risk
components.description: Dot + label + soft-fill pill. The eight-feet-glance affordance. Color is semantic; soft fill is the Lean palette tinted background.
components.css: .ds-health { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; gap: 6px; padding: 3px 10px; border-radius: 999px; font: 500 11px/1.4 var(--font-inter, 'Inter', system-ui, sans-serif); letter-spacing: -0.005em; } .ds-health-dot { display: inline-block; width: 5px; height: 5px; border-radius: 999px; } .ds-health-at-risk { background: #F7EBD5; color: #C99846; } .ds-health-at-risk .ds-health-dot { background: #C99846; }

components.name: Pipeline Status Badge — Clay
components.description: The pipeline status chip for active making stages (in_design, in_production, in_finishing). Uses the accent ramp to live parallel to — not on top of — health's green/amber/red.
components.css: .ds-status { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; gap: 6px; padding: 2px 10px; border-radius: 999px; font: 500 11px/1.4 var(--font-inter, 'Inter', system-ui, sans-serif); letter-spacing: -0.005em; } .ds-status-dot { display: inline-block; width: 5px; height: 5px; border-radius: 999px; } .ds-status-clay { background: #F2E4DC; color: #B86F52; } .ds-status-clay .ds-status-dot { background: #B86F52; }

components.name: Status Dot (primitive)
components.description: Standalone semantic colored dot. Spec §4.1 list-row leading affordance. Lives at shared/components/ui/StatusDot.tsx. Sizes sm/md/lg.
components.css: .ds-dot { display: inline-block; border-radius: 999px; vertical-align: middle; } .ds-dot-sm { width: 6px; height: 6px; } .ds-dot-md { width: 8px; height: 8px; } .ds-dot-lg { width: 10px; height: 10px; } .ds-dot-on-track { background: #6B8E5C; } .ds-dot-at-risk { background: #C99846; } .ds-dot-blocked { background: #B5544C; } .ds-dot-complete { background: #7A8B6F; } .ds-dot-paused { background: #9A968D; }

components.name: Hitlist Row (signature)
components.description: The default homepage row. Index + step text + blocker chip + sub-meta + dot + price + health pill. Reads from across the room.
components.html:
01
Slot on Toolpath CNC queue Toolpathdemo
Henderson Kitchen · Jessica Henderson · 3d to install
$42,800At risk
At Risk
components.css: .ds-hitlist-row { display: grid; grid-template-columns: 28px 1fr auto auto auto; align-items: center; gap: 14px; padding: 12px 22px; text-decoration: none; color: inherit; border-top: 1px solid rgba(26,25,22,0.06); transition: background 120ms cubic-bezier(0.2, 0, 0, 1); } .ds-hitlist-row:hover { background: rgba(184,111,82,0.04); } .ds-hr-idx { font: 400 11px/1 'JetBrains Mono', ui-monospace, monospace; color: var(--text-tertiary, #8B8782); text-align: center; font-variant-numeric: tabular-nums; } .ds-hr-step { font: 500 13.5px/1.4 var(--font-inter, 'Inter', system-ui, sans-serif); color: var(--text-primary, #1A1916); letter-spacing: -0.005em; display: flex; align-items: baseline; gap: 8px; } .ds-hr-sub { margin-top: 2px; font: 400 11.5px/1.4 var(--font-inter, 'Inter', system-ui, sans-serif); color: var(--text-tertiary, #8B8782); letter-spacing: -0.005em; } .ds-hr-price { font: 500 13px/1.2 'JetBrains Mono', ui-monospace, monospace; color: var(--text-primary, #1A1916); text-align: right; font-variant-numeric: tabular-nums; } .ds-hr-price small { display: block; font: 500 10px/1.2 var(--font-inter, 'Inter', system-ui, sans-serif); color: var(--text-tertiary, #8B8782); text-transform: uppercase; letter-spacing: 0.06em; margin-top: 2px; } .ds-status-amber { background: #F7EBD5; color: #C99846; } .ds-status-amber .ds-status-dot { background: #C99846; } .ds-hr-demo { display: inline-block; padding: 0 4px; margin-left: 4px; border-radius: 3px; background: rgba(26,25,22,0.08); color: var(--text-tertiary, #8B8782); font-size: 8.5px; letter-spacing: 0.04em; text-transform: uppercase; }

components.name: Demo Tag
components.description: Tiny chip beside a synthetic-data label. When BLOCKER_IS_SYNTHETIC is true, every blocker chip renders this. Drop the flag when real blocker fields land on Job.
components.css: .ds-demo-tag { display: inline-block; padding: 0 4px; border-radius: 3px; background: var(--surface-sunken, #ECE9E4); color: var(--text-tertiary, #8B8782); font: 600 9px/1.4 var(--font-inter, 'Inter', system-ui, sans-serif); letter-spacing: 0.04em; text-transform: uppercase; }

narrative.overview: A workshop foreman who never raises his voice. He knows every job's state before you ask, points at what needs you today, and stays out of the way when nothing's wrong. The dashboard inherits that posture: a near-white canvas with a barely-there warm glow at the foot, cards that float on soft shadow with no visible borders, serif display type for character, the Lean status palette held in the warm corner of the wheel. The Lit Workshop, lighter register: pure white where it counts, a hint of clay warmth at the edges to anchor the brand, sharp typographic precision throughout. The system rejects the SaaS-dashboard reflex (data-table-with-coloured-pills, gradient KPI cards, identical card grids, sidebar-icon-and-chart admin shell). It also rejects the construction-trade safety palette (yellow/orange/alarm-red), cabinet-incumbent gray density (Mozaik, ProKitchen, Cabinet Vision), and Bootstrap admin-template anonymity. The two reference systems that ARE allowed to influence this one: Claude desktop app and Apple Settings / iA Writer. Sharp, native-feel software, with a single warm clay accent that says workshop without saying hard-hat.
keyCharacteristics: Pure-white canvas (#FAFAF9) with a whisper-warm glow at the foot
No card borders by default; surfaces separate via shadow + tonal step
Serif display (Cormorant Garamond) for headings, italic for emphasis; Inter for body and data
Single clay accent (#B86F52), used as gradient fills more than as surface fills
Lean status palette held: muted sage, warm amber, dusty red, soft moss, gray-paused, andon-red
Dark pill CTAs (#1A1916), translucent segmented pills with backdrop-blur
Vertical-fade alpha gradients carry the transparent energy purposefully
Status visible at a glance from eight feet (spec §4.1 contract)

rules.name: The Foot-Glow Rule
rules.body: The canvas carries a vertical gradient: #FFFFFF at top, #FAFAF9 at 60%, rgba(184,111,82,0.04) at the foot. The glow is invisible at first glance and only registers as warmth on second look. If it reads as orange tint at first look, it's too strong. Tune until ambiguous.

rules.name: The Rare-Accent Rule
rules.body: Clay (#B86F52) appears at full saturation on ≤5% of any visible surface. It carries dots, gradient top-stops, and soft-fill pills. It does NOT carry buttons, surfaces, or large fills. The rarity is the point.

rules.name: The One Loud Red Rule
rules.body: Only --status-andon is allowed to be saturated. Every other status color is muted on purpose. If a screen needs an alert red, ask whether it's actually an andon. If not, use --status-blocked and a soft background.

rules.name: The Warm-Neutral Rule
rules.body: Every neutral tints warm (yellow/brown), never blue/gray. If a designer-tool default would push the neutral toward #F5F5F5 cool, that's wrong; the warm canvas tokens are the source of truth.

rules.name: The Serif-Italic Rule
rules.body: Italic Cormorant carries emphasis on 1-2 words inside a greeting or section title. Never on whole headings, never on body, never on UI labels. The italic is a brushstroke, not a default.

rules.name: The Tabular Number Rule
rules.body: Every numeric value that aligns in a column (currency, percentages, dates, job codes) gets font-variant-numeric: tabular-nums via the .tabular-nums utility class. Proportional figures only on running prose.

rules.name: The Never-700 Rule
rules.body: Heading weight is 500 in the serif, 600 in the sans for table-headers/eyebrows. Anything heavier reads as shout. The system is quiet by design.

rules.name: The Ghost-Border Rule
rules.body: Surfaces don't get borders. They earn separation through tonal step (the canvas is #FAFAF9, the card is #FFFFFF) plus a soft shadow at rest. If a designer asks should this card have a border, the answer is no.

rules.name: The Hover-Earns-Lift Rule
rules.body: Resting shadows are soft. Hover, drag, and focus deepen the shadow by ~80% (resting 0.10 → hover 0.18 on the same offset). Don't introduce color on hover; introduce lift.

rules.name: The Modal-Only-True-Elevation Rule
rules.body: The Modal / Drawer shadow vocabulary is reserved for genuinely lifted elements (modals, dropdowns, the andon banner). Cards don't get to use it; they stay at Resting or Hover.

dos: Do use the foot-glow on every page-level canvas (linear-gradient(180deg, #FFFFFF 0%, #FAFAF9 60%, rgba(184,111,82,0.04) 100%)). It's the brand signature.
Do float cards on shadow with no borders. Pure white cards on the #FAFAF9 canvas; resting shadow carries the lift.
Do use Cormorant Garamond 500 for all headings and the greeting. Italic only on 1-2 emphasis words inside a heading.
Do use dark ink pills (#1A1916) for primary CTAs. Clay accent is reserved for hover state, dots, gradient stops, and soft-fill pills.
Do put a colored status dot at the left edge of every list-view row (spec §4.1). The eight-feet glance test is the design contract.
Do render charts with vertical-fade alpha gradient fills (clay → transparent). It's the brand's transparency vocabulary, used purposefully.
Do render capacity / progress bars with horizontal gradient fills (clay(0.35) → clay(0.95)). Same vocabulary, different axis.
Do use tabular-nums on every numeric value that aligns in a column.
Do ensure 44×44px touch targets on shop-floor and install screens.
Do flag synthetic data with the demo tag. When real data lands, the flag goes away.
Do keep heading weight at 500 (Cormorant) / 600 (Inter all-caps labels). Never 700+.
donts: Don't add borders to cards. The Ghost-Border Rule applies system-wide. Borders are reserved for inputs, the rare emphasized divider, and inside-card row dividers at rgba(26,25,22,0.05).

Don't use the warm clay accent at full saturation on a surface (button background, card fill, banner). Clay shows up at full saturation only on small surfaces — dots, gradient top-stops, soft-fill pills. The Rare-Accent Rule.
Don't turn the foot-glow into an obvious orange band. If it reads as orange tint at first glance, it's too strong. Tune until ambiguous.
Don't use Cormorant Garamond for body, labels, buttons, table cells, or anything that isn't a heading or greeting.
Don't use italic Cormorant on whole headings. Italic is for 1-2 emphasis words inside a heading.

Don't use bright safety-green / safety-yellow / alarm-red. The status palette is muted sage / warm amber / dusty red on purpose. Only --status-andon is allowed to be loud, and only during active andon alerts. (Anti-reference: construction-trade safety palette.)
Don't use border-left or border-right greater than 1px as a coloured stripe. Use full borders, background tints, leading dots, or nothing.
Don't use gradient text. Use a solid color and weight.
Don't ship glassmorphism as default — purposeful translucency (the segmented pill, ambient glows) only. The remaining surfaces are solid.
Don't stack two information architectures on the same view (e.g., AI briefing card AND flat data list competing for the same eye). Pick one focal point.
Don't use display fonts for UI labels, buttons, or data. Cormorant is for headings only; Inter does everything else.
Don't reinvent standard affordances for flavor (custom scrollbars, weird form controls, non-standard modals).
Don't ship a Salesforce/NetSuite/ServiceNow data-table-with-pills aesthetic. (Anti-reference: generic enterprise SaaS.)
Don't ship a Mozaik/Cabinet Vision/ProKitchen gray slab UI. (Anti-reference: cabinet-industry incumbents.)
Don't ship a Procore/BuilderTrend safety-coloured construction-trade aesthetic. (Anti-reference: construction-trade apps.)
Don't ship a Bootstrap-admin / AdminLTE off-the-shelf shell. (Anti-reference: bootstrap admin templates.)
Don't show a hero-metric KPI card. KPIs live as header subtitles or as compact stats inside a card, not as big-number-with-gradient blocks.
Don't reach for a modal as the first answer. Exhaust inline / progressive disclosure first.
Don't introduce new tokens. DESIGN.md is canonical. New requirements update DESIGN.md; DESIGN.md updates the tokens.
